title: Are Revolve Group's (RVLV) Stronger Q1 Numbers Clarifying Its Long-Term Earnings Power Story?
text: Revolve Group reported stronger Q1 2026 numbers. Net sales for Q1 2026 were $343 million, an increase of 16% year-over-year, and the company reported revenue growing 15.6% year-on-year to $342.9 million with earnings per share of $0.20, both surpassing analysts' consensus estimates. Revolve Group shares edged up 3% recently, with investors appearing to take a fresh look at the company as retail sector sentiment shifts and fashion e-commerce players compete for market share. However, sentiment has been mixed. Shares fell 16.2% in the afternoon session after the company's first-quarter results, while beating Wall Street expectations, failed to ease concerns about its long-term growth trajectory. Despite strong quarterly results, there are concerns about long-term growth. The company has shown sluggish compounded annual sales growth of just 5% over the last three years, and Wall Street's forward-looking revenue growth estimates of 6.6% remain below the sector average. Additionally, the company's price-to-earnings ratio stands at 33.8x, noticeably higher than both the US Specialty Retail industry average of 17.3x and its peer average of 15.1x.
